title: Bacopa scoparioides
intro: Bacopa scoparioides, also known as Bacopa, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Bacopaceae, traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is believed to possess neuroprotective, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to enhance cognitive function, memory, and mood, as well as support overall brain health. The bioactive constituents of Bacopa scoparioides include bacosides, which are a group of triterpenoid saponins, along with other compounds such as alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids. Bacopa is commonly prepared as a herbal supplement in capsule or tablet form, as well as in the form of an extract, often standardized to contain a specific percentage of bacosides.
